input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **Visual Studio Code** (or **VS for Mac** replacement: JetBrains Rider) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) | IDE |
| **C# Dev Kit extension** | VS Code marketplace | C#/MAUI support in VS Code |

### 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all dependent workloads (Android SDK, AOT compilers, etc.).

### 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See installed SDK versions
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads & available updates
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload search maui

# Check Xcode version (must meet Apple's minimum for your target iOS)
xcodebuild -version

# Accept Xcode license (required)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

Check the [.NET MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/platform/support/policy/maui) for which .NET SDK versions ship with MAUI and which Xcode/Android SDK levels are supported.

### 4. Android-Specific Setup

The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK automatically under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`. To manage emulators:

```bash
# Install an Android emulator image (example)
dotnet android sdk install "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"

# Create an AVD
dotnet android avd create --name Pixel_API34 --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

Or install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for its AVD Manager GUI.

### 5. iOS-Specific Setup

```bash
# Boot an iOS simulator
open -a Simulator

# Or list available simulators
xcrun simctl list devices
```

No additional install needed beyond Xcode.

### 6. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create and run a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for all targets
dotnet build

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios
```

### 7. Quick Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `workload install` fails | Run with `sudo`; check `dotnet --version` ≥ 8.0 |
| iOS build fails | `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`; verify `xcode-select -p` points to Xcode.app |
| Android emulator won't start | Ensure Apple Silicon-compatible image (`arm64-v8a`); grant permissions in System Settings → Privacy |
| "No installed provisioning profiles" | Use simulator first; for devices, enroll in Apple Developer Program |

**TL;DR order of operations:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → create project → run.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They don't need me to run commands or explore a codebase — they need practical guidance. Let me provide a concise but complete answer.
